channel.title: Maths on Vasileios Balntas (vbalnt)
channel.description: Recent content in Maths on Vasileios Balntas (vbalnt)

item.title: Growing Trees with Grammar: An Introduction to L-Systems
item.description:
Nature is full of self-similar patterns. The branching of trees, the spiral of shells, the fractal coastlines. In 1968, botanist Aristid Lindenmayer discovered a beautiful way to describe these patterns using simple string rewriting rules. These L-systems (Lindenmayer systems) can generate stunningly complex structures from minimal rules.

L-Systems are rewriting systems.

A rewriting system is a formal method where you repeatedly replace parts of a structure according to a fixed set of rules. Think of it like a find-and-replace that runs forever: start with a simple seed, apply the rules, and watch complexity emerge. L-systems apply this idea to strings of symbols, which we then interpret as drawing instructions.
